Items.Title: 在 React 中构建搜索和过滤功能🦄 | Constructing Search & Filter Functionality in React 🦄
Items.Description:
劳伦泰森🕶

发表于搜索和过滤功能很简单，但我经常需要参考文档。 要记住的最重要的事情是组件之间的信息流——状态在哪里，它在哪里传递，以及通过回调函数将数据从子组件发送到其父组件。 起初，这个功能可能感觉像是一个令人困惑的循环，但在你构建了几次这样的组件之后，基本步骤就变得更容易记住了。

最近我从头开始构建了一个 React 应用程序作为编码 Bootcamp 项目。 该应用程序名为 Scientific Foodie，这是一个共享有关所有不同类型食物（如水果、蔬菜、谷物、葫芦等）的科学信息的数据库：

因为我正在构建一个数据库，所以我决定提供搜索和过滤功能，以便用户可以与数据库进行最充分的交互。

在这个应用程序中，我的搜索和过滤器组件协同工作，所以下面，我将介绍构建每个组件的步骤，以及我如何（这里有一个提示）将它们链接在一起。

由于我的 Search 和 Filter 组件是我的 FoodContainer 组件的兄弟和子组件，我确定 FoodContainer 将保持状态并处理回调函数。 为什么？ 因为兄弟组件不能直接相互传递数据。 数据只能在父母和孩子之间上下流动。 因此，它们的父组件 FoodContainer 将保持状态并处理整体功能。

制作你的过滤器组件📌

一旦您编写了基本的 JSX，即您过滤的内容，就该构建功能了。 过滤器选项的值应与您过滤的数据键相匹配。 我的应用程序按组过滤我的食物。

  1. 在您的父组件中，创建将捕获用户选择的过滤器的状态。 我喜欢将过滤器的默认状态设置为“全部”，这是过滤器组件中的第一个选项。 接下来，我们将创建一个简单的回调函数来处理捕获 event.target.value，即过滤器更改和更新状态。 最初，我总是将我的辅助函数设置为控制台记录选择，这样我就知道我的回调正在工作——稍后会详细介绍。

  2. 将辅助函数作为道具从您的父组件传递给您的子组件，即过滤器组件。 这允许回调由与调用它的组件不同的组件拥有。

  3. 过滤器功能需要一个 onChange 事件。 只需将辅助函数作为道具分配给 onChange。 每次选择过滤器选项时，该值都会通过我们的辅助函数传递给我们的父组件。

  4. 试试这个以确保它有效！ 你怎么知道？ 请记住，我们在父组件的辅助函数中有一个临时控制台日志。 在您的应用程序中，选择一个过滤器选项并查看控制台中记录的值。 同样，确保您的选项值与您的数据匹配。

  5. 既然您知道回调有效，请删除控制台日志并将其替换为您的状态的 setter 函数。 并添加一个临时控制台日志来检查您的状态变量的值。 在您的应用程序中，再次选择一个过滤器，您将看到您的状态在控制台中捕获过滤器的变化！ 最后，删除控制台日志。

制作你的搜索组件🔎

暂时将过滤器组件放在一边，让我们构建我们的搜索。

  1. 在顶级父组件中设置状态，在我的例子中是 FoodContainer。 您的搜索状态的默认值应该是一个空字符串。

  2. 创建一个简单的回调函数来处理在搜索栏中输入的内容。 正如我们最初使用过滤器辅助函数所做的那样，将您的搜索辅助函数设置为控制台记录搜索栏中的文本。 我们要确保它有效。

  3. 接下来，您的 Search 组件应该采用两个 props，即搜索状态变量和辅助函数。 我们将状态作为道具传递，因为我们正在构建一个受控组件。

  4. 在您的 Search 组件中，该值应将搜索状态变量作为道具，而您的 onChange 将辅助回调作为道具。 您的助手正在捕获 event.target.value。

  5. 试试这个以确保它有效！ 在我们的父组件中使用临时控制台登录我们的搜索助手功能，在您的搜索栏中输入一些内容。 在控制台中，您将看到每次击键记录为一个字母。 没关系！ 我们还没有更新状态； 我们只是控制台日志记录以确保回调正常工作。

  6. 接下来，删除该控制台日志并将其替换为搜索状态的 setter 函数。 然后再添加一个临时控制台日志以检查您的状态是否正确更新并在搜索栏中再次输入。 您将在控制台中看到文本，并在搜索栏中看到单词形成。 凉爽的！ 因为这是一个将状态分配为搜索值的受控组件，所以字母会在您键入时出现在搜索栏中，也就是用户正在实时观看状态更新。

  7. 最后，删除最后一个控制台日志。

将过滤器和搜索链接在一起⛓

最后一步是根据搜索和过滤的内容更新页面上显示的项目。 就我而言，我希望我的用户根据食物的通用名称在数据库中搜索特定食物，按食物组过滤，然后一起执行这些操作。

我的父组件 FoodContainer 处理在页面上显示我的食物，所以我添加了以下代码：

  1. 声明保存要映射的数组的变量。 我最初映射了我的整个食物数组，但该数组没有内置我的搜索和过滤逻辑。所以，我创建了一个变量来处理我的搜索和过滤； 这个数组将被映射以显示我的项目。

  2. 写一个 if 语句。 如果选择的过滤器是“All”，那么数组中的所有元素都将返回。 这写为return true 。 否则，返回与用户选择的过滤器匹配的元素。

我们可以在这里停下来； 但是，我们希望搜索和过滤器一起工作，以便用户可以同时使用两者。 还有几个步骤：

  1. 接下来，链接另一个过滤器。 现在我们的变量foodsToDisplay将过滤器和搜索代码都考虑在内，而不是其中之一。 链式方法本质上就是在一个语句中一个接一个地调用方法，让你的代码简洁，逻辑可以继续。

  2. 就我而言，我允许用户按通用名称搜索食物。 添加includes是因为我想在用户键入时返回部分匹配项。 最后，我将通用名称和搜索转换为小写，这样大写就不会妨碍。

现在，最后，让我们映射新数组！ 完成后，尝试在您的应用中进行搜索和过滤。 它太酷了。

那么它对我来说怎么样？ 这是我的 React 项目的结果：

毛雷尔·克里斯蒂安 - 一旦暂停，laurentyson85 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，laurentyson85 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，laurentyson85 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 laurentyson85 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 laurentyson85 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 laurentyson85 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

LaurenTyson🕶

Posted on Aug 30

Search and filter functionality is straightforward, yet I often need to refer back to documentation. The biggest thing to remember is the information flow between the components - where does state live, where it's passed, and sending data from a child component to its parent via a callback function. At first, this functionality can feel like a confusing loop, but after you construct components like these a few times, the basic steps get easier to remember.

Recently I built a React app from scratch as a coding Bootcamp project. The app is called Scientific Foodie, a database that shares scientific information about all different types of food, such as fruits, vegetables, grains, gourds, and more:

Because I was building a database, I decided to have search and filter functionality so users could interact with the database to the fullest.

In this app, my Search and Filter components work in tandem, so below, I walk through the steps of constructing each component and how I, and here's a hint, chained them together.

And since my Search and Filter components are siblings and children of my FoodContainer component, I determined that FoodContainer would hold state and handle the callback functions. Why? Because sibling components can't pass data to each other directly. Data can only flow up and down between parent and child. Therefore their parent component, FoodContainer, will hold state and handle the overall functionality.

Making your Filter Component 📌

Once you have the basic JSX written, i.e., what you're filtering by, it's time to construct the functionality. The value of your filter options should match the data key you're filtering. My app filters my food by group.

  1. In your parent component, create state that will capture the user's selected filter. I like to set default state for my filter to "All," which is the first option in my Filter component. "All" ensures everything displays upon page load, and while filtering, the user can return to seeing everything at any point.

  2. Next, we'll create a simple callback function to handle capturing the event.target.value, which is the filter change, and updating state. Initially, I always set my helper function to console log the selection, just so I know my callback is working — more on this in a minute.

  3. Pass the helper function as props from your parent component to your child, the Filter component. This allows the callback to be owned by a different component than the one invoking it.

  4. Filter functionality takes an onChange event. Simply assign the helper function as props to onChange. Each time a filter option is selected, the value is passed up through our helper function to our parent component.

  5. Try this out to make sure it works! How will you know? Remember, we have a temporary console log in the helper function in our parent component. In your app, select a filter option and see the value logged in your console. Again, make sure your option values match your data.

  6. Now that you know the callback works remove the console log and replace it with your state's setter function. And add one more temporary console log to check the value of your state variable. In your app, select a filter again, and you'll see your state capturing the change in filters in the console! Finally, remove the console log.

Making your Search Component 🔎

Leaving the Filter component aside, for now, let's construct our Search.

  1. Set up state in the top-level parent component, in my case FoodContainer. The default value for your search state should be an empty string.

  2. Create a simple callback function to handle what's typed in the search bar. As we initially did with our filter helper function, set your search helper function to console log the text in the search bar. We want to make sure it works.

  3. Next, your Search component should take two props, the search state variable, and the helper function. We're passing state down as props because we're constructing a controlled component.

  4. In your Search component, the value should take the search state variable as props, and your onChange takes the helper callback as props. Your helper is capturing the event.target.value.

  5. Try this out to make sure it works! With the temporary console log in our search helper function in our parent component, type something into your search bar. In the console, you'll see each keystroke logged as a single letter at a time. That's ok! We're not updating state just yet; we're only console logging to ensure the callback is working correctly.

  6. Next, remove that console log and replace it with the search state's setter function. Then add one more temporary console log to check that your state is updating correctly and type again in the search bar. You'll see the text in the console and the word forming in the search bar. Cool! Because this is a controlled component with state assigned as the search value, the letters appear in the search bar as you type, aka the user is watching state update in real-time.

  7. Finally, remove that last console log.

Chaining Filter and Search together ⛓

The last step is updating the items displayed on the page based on what's being searched and filtered. In my case, I want my users to search for a specific food in the database based on the food's common name, filter by food group, and do these actions together.

My parent component FoodContainer handles displaying my food on the page, so that's where I added the following code:

  1. Declare the variable that holds the array you'll map over. I originally mapped my entire foods array, but that array didn't have my search and filter logic built in. So, I created a variable that handled my search and filter; this array will be mapped to display my items instead.

  2. Write an if statement. If the selected filter is "All," then all of the elements in the array will return. This is written as return true. Otherwise, return the elements that match the user's selected filter.

We could stop here; however, we want search and filter to work together so the user can utilize both. A couple more steps:

  1. Next, chain on another filter. Now our variable, foodsToDisplay, takes both the filter and the search code into consideration instead of one or the other. Chaining methods is essentially calling methods one after another in one statement, allowing your code to be concise and the logic to continue.

  2. In my case, I'm allowing users to search for foods by common name. includes is added because I want to return partial matches as the user types. Finally, I'm converting the common name and the search to lowercase so capitalization doesn't get in the way.

TLDR 😎 - either the user searches through all the foods or searches a filtered selection.

Now, finally, let's map over the new array! Once done, try out search and filtering in your app. It's pretty cool.

So how did it go for me? Here's how my React project turned out:

Maurer Krisztián - Aug 26
